# Tangent ratio in right triangles

## Short Teaching Summary
In a right triangle, the tangent of an angle is the ratio of the length of the side opposite the angle to the length of the side adjacent to the angle.

## Difficulty
0.00

## Related Concepts
- Sine in right triangle (analogy_for)

## Misconceptions
- None recorded.

## Best Examples
- None recorded.

## Best Analogy
- Use the tangent (tan θ) ratio when a problem involves the Opposite and Adjacent sides.
